originalTitle: Toddlers among more than 50 schoolchildren kidnapped in Nigeria
title: Helping Our Friends in Nigeria
content:
Hello from Your Africa Reporter!

Hello, my little friends! It’s your friendly news reporter from Africa here. I hope you are having a wonderful day, learning and playing. Today, I want to talk about something important that happened in a country not too far away from many of us. It’s a bit of a sad story, but it’s important for us to know and send our kind thoughts.

A Big Country Called Nigeria

Our story takes us to a big, big country in West Africa called Nigeria. Did you know? Nigeria has so many people living there, more than any other country in Africa! It's a land full of colourful markets, amazing music, and busy cities. Many children in Nigeria go to school every day, just like you, to learn new things and play with their friends. Nigeria has many different parts, and one part is called Borno State. You can think of a 'state' like a very big neighbourhood in a country. In Borno State, there is a small town called Mussa. Mussa town is a place where families live, children play, and people go about their daily lives.

What Happened in Mussa Town?

Something happened recently in Mussa town that made many grown-ups very sad and worried. Some bad people came and took away more than 50 schoolchildren from their school. Imagine, some of these children were very, very small, like toddlers! That means they were just learning to walk and talk, maybe even younger than some of you. When someone takes children away without their parents' permission, we call it 'kidnapping.' This is a very wrong thing to do. Children should always be safe, especially when they are at school. School is a place for learning and fun, not for worry. No one has said, “We did this!” yet. We don't know who the bad people are or why they did such a thing. But what we do know is that many children are now away from their homes and their families. This makes their parents and friends feel very, very sad and worried. They miss their little ones so much.

Why Are Schools So Important?

Did you know? Going to school is super important for every child! It's where you learn your ABCs and 123s. You get to draw pictures, read exciting stories, and learn about the world around you. School is also where you meet your friends and play games together. For children in Mussa town, going to school was their everyday adventure. They looked forward to seeing their teachers and classmates. Now, some of these children are not there, and their classrooms feel empty without them. Their friends miss playing with them during break time. Every child has a right to go to school and feel safe. Grown-ups everywhere try their best to make sure schools are happy and safe places for all children. This is why what happened in Mussa town is so upsetting to many people.

People Are Helping

Even though this is a sad story, there is also hope. Many grown-ups are working very hard to bring the children back home safely. The police and the army in Nigeria are looking everywhere for the children. They want to find them and make sure they are safe. Many other people, not just in Nigeria but all over Africa and the world, are thinking about these children and their families. They are sending good wishes and prayers. They want the children to come back quickly and be reunited with their moms, dads, brothers, and sisters. Did you know? When something sad happens, it's good for people to come together and help each other. That's what grown-ups are doing now. They are trying their very best to solve this problem and make things right again for the children of Mussa town.

Sending Hope and Good Wishes

It's important for us to remember that most schools are very safe places. What happened in Mussa town is a very rare and wrong thing. We must always believe that good people will work hard to help those in need. Let's all send our kindest thoughts and wishes to the children who were taken and to their families in Nigeria. We hope they will be found soon and brought back home to their loved ones. We hope they can go back to their classrooms, play with their friends, and learn new things again very soon. Let's think of them and hope for a happy return. Thank you for listening, my little friends. Remember to always be kind and spread love!
summary: More than 50 schoolchildren, including very young ones, were taken from their school in Mussa town, Nigeria, making many people sad, but grown-ups are working hard to bring them home safely.
whyMatters: This is important because every child should be safe at school and at home, and many people are helping to make sure these children can come back to their families and continue learning.

originalTitle: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
title: Senior IS leader killed in joint operation, US and Nigeria say
content: President Donald Trump said the operation eliminated "the most active terrorist in the world", Abu-Bilal al-Minuki.

originalTitle: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
title: New outbreak of Ebola kills 80 in eastern DR Congo
content: Africa's top health agency says around 246 cases have been reported - a case has also been reported in Uganda.

originalTitle: Tens of thousands join far-right rally in central London
title: Thousands of Friends Walk Together in a Big City!
content:
Hello, Super Friends!

Hello, super friends! Have you ever seen lots and lots of people all gathered in one place? Maybe at a big school event, or a fun party, or even a market day with many families? It's pretty amazing to see so many people together, isn't it? Well, something very much like that happened in a huge, busy city called London, which is far, far away in a country called England.

What Happened in London?

On one sunny Saturday, a very special day, many, many thousands of people came together in the middle of London. Imagine a street full of people, like a very, very long line stretching as far as your eyes could see! It was like a giant walking parade, but instead of colorful floats and music, these people were walking and talking and holding signs. They were all there to share their thoughts and ideas about things that are important to them. Did you know? London is the capital city of England! It's one of the biggest cities in the whole wide world, with lots of famous landmarks like big clocks, tall towers, and even a palace where the King lives!

Why Do People Walk Together?

Sometimes, people want to tell everyone about something that they care about very, very much. They walk together in a big group to show how many people share the same feelings or ideas. It's like when you and your friends all love the same game, and you want to tell everyone how much fun it is! Or maybe you want to show that you care about keeping our planet clean, so you all walk together to pick up litter. When many people walk together like this, it's a way for them to use their voices. They want others to hear what they are thinking and feeling. It's important for people to be able to share their ideas, just like it's important for you to share your ideas in class or with your family.

A Special Organizer

A person named Tommy helped to organize this big walk. When lots of people want to do something together, it's helpful to have someone who can help everyone know where to go and what to do. Tommy was one of the people who helped make sure everyone knew how to gather and walk through the city safely. It takes a lot of planning to get so many people to move together! Did you know? When many people gather together in one place, we call it a 'crowd'! Crowds can be for fun things like concerts, sports games, or even just lots of shoppers in a big store. This was a very, very big crowd!

Different Ideas, Different Thoughts

In our big, wonderful world, everyone is unique and special. Everyone has different favorite colors, different favorite foods, and different ideas about how things should be. Some people like to eat bananas, and some people prefer apples! And that's okay! It's good to have lots of different ideas because it helps us learn and grow. Sometimes, people have very strong ideas about how they think things should work in their country or in the world. This big walk in London was for people who had some very strong ideas that they wanted to share with everyone else. They wanted to make sure their voices were heard loudly and clearly.

Being Kind and Listening

Even when people have big walks and share big ideas, it's super, super important to always remember to be kind and friendly to everyone. We should try our best to listen to each other with our ears and understand what others are saying, even if their ideas are different from ours. Everyone wants to feel safe, happy, and respected, right? It's like playing with your friends. You might not always agree on which game to play, but you can still be kind to each other and try to find a way to play together happily. Listening to others helps us understand them better, and understanding helps us all live together peacefully in our big world. Did you know? There are many different ways people share their ideas! They can talk, write letters, draw pictures, sing songs, or even march together peacefully like these people did in London!

Our Wonderful World of People

Our world is like a big, colorful rainbow, full of different people from different places, with different thoughts, different smiles, and different dreams. Every single person is special, like a unique color in that rainbow. And when we all try to understand and be kind to each other, our world becomes even more beautiful and strong. So, on that day in London, many thousands of people went for a big walk to share their voices and their ideas. It reminds us that our world is big and full of people who want to share their thoughts and feelings. And the best way for all of us to live in this big, wonderful world is with kindness, by listening carefully, and by trying to be friends with everyone, no matter how different our ideas might be. Let's all remember to be kind, listen well, and always be friendly!
summary: Many thousands of people gathered in London for a big walk to share their important ideas, reminding us that it's good to share our voices while always being kind and listening to everyone.
whyMatters: This matters because it shows how people use their voices to share what they believe, and it teaches us that even when people have different ideas, it's always important to be kind, listen, and treat everyone with respect in our big world.

title: Alonso reaches agreement in principle to be next Chelsea boss
